sglang.title: Fix TRTLLM target verify query metadata
sglang.author: merrymercy
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary

Remove a target-verify metadata override that forced the query length to the configured draft token count.
Let target-verify graph metadata keep the query length derived from the captured request shape.

Verification

python3 -m py_compile python/sglang/srt/layers/attention/trtllm_mha_backe

sglang.title: Add all_to_all_single to GroupCoordinator
sglang.author: merrymercy
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary

Add all_to_all_single method to GroupCoordinator with a reg_all_to_all_single custom op wrapper, following the same pattern as existing reduce_scatter_tensor.
This enables all-to-all sequence parallelism support for models that manage SP at the model level.

Original commits

51821cb7a

DeepSeek-R1-MXFP4-Preview — TP=4, EP=4,

sglang.title: [Spec] Clamp multimodal pad sentinels in spec-v2 draft prefill embedding
sglang.author: hnyls2002
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary

Spec-v2 draft prefill fed out-of-vocab multimodal pad sentinels straight into the draft embed_tokens, causing an out-of-bounds embedding gather.
Clamp the ids in the draft embed (like the main model already does), and probe next_token_ids so a genuinely corrupted id still surfaces.

Root ca

Motivation
Dual-chunk sparse prefill can hit an empty local sparse selection after global vertical/slash top-k indices are split into intra, successor, and inter chunk stages.
The existing fallback for empty selections sampled roughly five indices with torch.arange(..., k_len / 5), independent of th

sglang.title: Add parallel-rank dump filenames and pipeline-global layer remapping to dumper
sglang.author: fzyzcjy

Two small, independent quality-of-life improvements to srt/debug_utils/dumper.py for dumping models under parallelism.
Changes

include_parallel_rank_in_filename option (default off): when enabled, appends parallel-rank tags (pp_rank/tp_rank/cp_rank/ep_rank/etp_rank) gathered from the fra

sglang.title: Speed up dump comparator percentile computation using numpy
sglang.author: fzyzcjy
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
_compute_percentiles in the tensor comparator called torch.quantile once
per percentile, and torch.quantile sorts the full tensor on every call. For
the large tensors the comparator typically handles, this is needlessly slow.
Modifications

Compute all DEFAULT_PERCENTILES in a single np.p

sglang.title: Avoid calling filter_batch with chunked_req_to_exclude being things unrelated to chunked reqs
sglang.author: fzyzcjy
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
What
In ScheduleBatch.retract_all, replace the terminal self.filter_batch(retracted_reqs) call with self.reqs = []. Behavior is unchanged.
Why
retract_all was passing retracted_reqs (which is just an alias for self.reqs) as filter_batch's chunked_req_to_exclude parameter. That parameter is meant to

sglang.title: Extract release_req and retract_all as module-level free functions
sglang.author: fzyzcjy
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
We want to release a Req that is not currently sitting inside a ScheduleBatch (e.g. a req picked off another queue, or a req in a context where building a fake batch just to reuse this logic is wrong). Today, release_req and retract_all are methods on ScheduleBatch, so they require an own

sglang.title: Refactor Req.fill_ids into full_untruncated_fill_ids + fill_len with equivalence
sglang.author: fzyzcjy
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Split the dual-phase Req.fill_ids field into a stored full sequence + integer marker, as a strictly mechanical, byte-equivalent refactor.
The OLD fill_ids field had two phases within a single iter:

init_next_round_input wrote the full untruncated form (origin + output [+ DLLM mask block]

sglang.title: Fix Req fill_len (fill_ids) having dual semantics by restricting to truncated/committed semantics
sglang.author: fzyzcjy
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
After #26637 split Req.fill_ids into (full_untruncated_fill_ids, fill_len), the integer marker fill_len still carried the dual-phase semantics of the OLD len(fill_ids). This PR pins fill_len to a single meaning: the truncated length of the fill_ids view — the boundary up to which full_unt

vllm.title: [Dependency] Remove stale cuDNN frontend upper bound
vllm.author: mmangkad
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
Remove the stale upper bound on nvidia-cudnn-frontend since the breaking changes in 1.19.0 have been fixed. The constraint is now >=1.19.1, which excludes 1.19.0 while letting vLLM pick up newer cuDNN frontend fixes and improvements. This is compatible with FlashInfer's >=1.13.0 requirement.

pip3 install torch==2.11.0 --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/xpu
